subject: Chemistry
title: Al-Razi: The Skeptical Alchemist
description: Learn Al-Razi: The Skeptical Alchemist in one minute.

hook.text: Al-Razi was the first alchemist to classify chemicals into categories: earths, metals, salts, and spirits.

buildup.text: Working in 9th-century Persia, he wrote 'The Secret of Secrets'—a practical lab manual, not mystical poetry.

discovery.text: He classified substances by how they behaved: what dissolved, what evaporated, what reacted with acids.

twist.text: Al-Razi rejected the idea of the philosopher's stone. He focused on real, reproducible experiments—centuries ahead of his time.

climax.text: He also developed plaster of Paris for setting broken bones and was the first to distill kerosene from petroleum.

punchline.text: Rejected magic, demanded proof. A scientist born a millennium too early.

quiz.question: How was Al-Razi different from other alchemists of his era?
options: He rejected the philosopher's stone and focused on reproducible experiments
He worked exclusively with gold transmutation
He refused to write down any of his findings
